name: GraphQL queries executable over HTTP GET
description: Sends a GraphQL query in the URL query string over HTTP GET (/graphql?query={__typename}) and checks it executes. When the server runs GraphQL operations over GET, the operation and (for queries) its result travel in the URL — so they land in proxy / CDN caches, browser history, and access logs, and the endpoint becomes CSRF-reachable (a state-changing operation triggered by a crafted / if GET mutations are also allowed). OWASP's GraphQL guidance is to accept operations over POST only. The __typename probe is schema-agnostic (it resolves on every GraphQL schema), so the finding is about the transport, independent of what the schema exposes. The predicate requires a successful data response, so a server that rejects GET (405 / errors-only) does not trip it.

vulnerability.remediation: Accept GraphQL operations over POST only, and reject GET (or restrict GET to safe, explicitly-allow-listed persisted queries). ASP.NET Core HotChocolate: `app.MapGraphQL().WithOptions(new GraphQLServerOptions { AllowedGetOperations = AllowedGetOperations.None })` (or disable the GET middleware). Never allow mutations over GET. Verify with `curl -k 'https://host/graphql?query=%7B__typename%7D'` — a POST-only server returns 405 or an error, not a `data` result.
